content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 5.1.1(i) WARN — Privacy policy states "We do not collect, process, or store your location data," but `RunSessionManager.swift:1-19` imports CoreLocation and calls `startUpdatingLocation()`, and the description advertises "maps every run with GPS."
Pierre: Apple cares because a privacy policy that directly contradicts observable code behavior is a classic 5.1.1(i) rejection trigger and erodes user trust. Here the app clearly tracks GPS routes (CoreLocation + "maps every run with GPS" in the description), yet the policy explicitly denies collecting any location data. Update the privacy policy to accurately disclose location collection/usage (e.g. on-device route tracking and any iCloud sync).
